Objective: Confirm the order is deliverable and the customer is scheduled correctly.
Start Trigger: Order is released from sales or ecommerce into operations.
Completion Condition: Appointment is confirmed and the order is cleared for pre-delivery review.
Required Inputs: SKU details; customer contact info; install requirements; haul-away request; address data.
Systems / Tools: OMS; scheduling workflow; customer contact records
Happy Path Summary: The order enters ops cleanly; service attributes are present; the customer is contacted; and the order is scheduled without downstream conflict.
Pain Point 1: Order data conflicts with install reality.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the conflict is minor and correctable or a real serviceability problem.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Bad appointments; failed installs; wasted route capacity; customer distrust.
Pain Point 2: Install requirements or haul-away scope are missing or miscoded.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the missing detail changes crew type; parts; or appointment length.
Pain Point 3: Address or customer contact data is incomplete or wrong.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether the issue can be corrected before route planning.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Routing errors; missed contact; failed first attempt.
Common Exceptions: Customer unavailable for confirmation; missing service attributes; conflicting order notes.
Rare Edge Cases: Order sold as installable when site/service is actually out of scope.
Escalation Trigger: Order data creates ambiguity about what can actually be delivered or installed.
Required Documentation: Corrected service attributes; contact outcomes; schedule commitments; OMS and scheduling record updates.
Proof Required: Updated notes and corrected order attributes.

Objective: Verify the home; hookups; access path; and product specs match the install.
Completion Condition: Readiness is verified or exceptions are flagged.
Required Inputs: Order details; specs; photos; customer notes; utility requirements.
Systems / Tools: Photos; notes; readiness checklist; specs; utility requirements; reason codes
Happy Path Summary: The team verifies the site remotely; identifies no blocking issues; captures the right notes; and passes a clean job forward to route planning.
Pain Point 1: Wrong appliance specs or missing customer photos.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the mismatch can be resolved before dispatch with better photos; spec confirmation; or a product swap.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Wrong crew/tools/product sent; failed delivery; route waste; rescheduling; lower customer trust.
Pain Point 2: The customer says hookups or clearance exist when they do not.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the gap can be fixed by simple customer prep or makes install impossible that day.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Crew arrives to a non-installable site; route capacity loss; rework; customer frustration.
Pain Point 3: Access constraints like stairs; tight turns; or door width are discovered too late.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether the crew can still complete safely with different equipment; added labor; or a revised plan.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Injury; property damage; failed install; disruption to remaining stops.
Common Exceptions: Stacked units not disclosed; blocked shutoff valves; customers missing required parts.
Rare Edge Cases: Gas leak signs; unsafe wiring; water damage.
Escalation Trigger: Safety; code; or cost decisions exceed field/front-line authority.
Required Documentation: Photos; notes; reason codes; customer acknowledgment; readiness status updates.
Proof Required: Photos; notes; reason codes; customer acknowledgment.
KPI 2: Avoided failed installs / exception catch rate

Objective: Build a feasible route and make sure the correct product; parts; and tools are loaded.
Start Trigger: Site readiness is confirmed or exceptions are routed.
Completion Condition: Route is assigned and the load is verified against the jobs.
Required Inputs: Confirmed install notes; crew capacity; geography; inventory availability; accessories; service windows.
Supporting Roles: Warehouse/load team; pre-install support; crew lead
Systems / Tools: Dispatch dashboard; route planning tools; inventory/load verification
Happy Path Summary: Dispatch builds a route that matches promised windows; crew capability; and actual inventory/tool needs.
Pain Point 1: Product; part; or accessory shortage is discovered at load verification.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the shortage has a same-day substitute or forces a replan.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Failed stop; wasted crew time; customer disappointment; route disruption.
Pain Point 2: Route looks feasible on paper but not with actual service times or site complexity.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the overload is absorbable with resequencing or requires route reduction.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Late arrivals; missed windows; compressed installs; cascading service failure.
Pain Point 3: Install notes from readiness review do not make it into dispatch/load decisions.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether critical notes change tools; labor; or stop sequencing.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Wrong tools/crew; unsafe field improvisation; failed completion.
Common Exceptions: Short inventory; missing install kits; accessory mismatch.
Rare Edge Cases: Product loaded to the wrong route; critical shortage discovered after departure.
Escalation Trigger: Conflicts or shortages exceed dispatch authority.
Required Documentation: Load verification; shortages; resequencing decisions; route assignment and manifest updates.
Proof Required: Verified load record and route notes.

Objective: Confirm the site matches pre-delivery assumptions and that safe access exists.
Completion Condition: Crew makes a go/no-go decision for entry and install.
Required Inputs: Order packet; site notes; customer presence; actual home conditions.
Systems / Tools: Order packet; site notes; customer contact method; field documentation
Happy Path Summary: The crew finds the site as described; confirms access; and proceeds into removal/install without delay.
Pain Point 1: Actual access path does not match readiness information.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the mismatch is still safe with different equipment or a revised plan.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Injury; property damage; failed stop; route disruption.
Pain Point 2: Customer is not ready or not fully present on arrival.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the issue is solvable within route tolerance.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Long dwell time; missed windows; failed delivery.
Pain Point 3: Site conditions conflict with service sold.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether the conflict is a minor field workaround or a true no-go.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Unauthorized work; damage; incomplete install; complaint escalation.
Common Exceptions: Gate/access problems; customer not fully ready; last-minute site mismatch.
Rare Edge Cases: Unsafe entry conditions or structural/safety hazards.
Escalation Trigger: Site conditions do not match the order and the crew cannot safely proceed.
Required Documentation: Actual conditions; mismatch notes; photos; stop status updates.

Objective: Remove the old unit safely when haul-away is included.
Completion Condition: Old unit is removed or an exception is documented.
Required Inputs: Haul-away request; shutoff access; safety conditions; equipment; disposal rules.
Systems / Tools: Haul-away request; safety checklist; disposal rules
Happy Path Summary: The old unit is disconnected; moved safely; protected against damage; and loaded for disposal or haul-away.
Pain Point 1: Shutoff access or disconnection conditions are not as expected.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether safe disconnection is possible within crew authority.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Damage; leak; unsafe condition; failed install.
Pain Point 2: Haul-away scope is disputed at the door.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the service was sold and documented clearly.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Customer dispute; extended dwell time; complaint.
Pain Point 3: Removal path creates damage or injury risk.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether alternate equipment or labor makes the move safe.
Common Exceptions: Old unit not disconnected; haul-away misunderstanding.
Rare Edge Cases: Unsafe utility condition uncovered during removal.
Escalation Trigger: Damage; safety; or scope dispute exceeds crew authority.
Required Documentation: Condition of old unit area; scope dispute notes; damage notes; stop notes and reason codes.

Objective: Place and connect the new unit correctly and to code.
Start Trigger: After removal or once the install area is clear.
Completion Condition: Appliance is connected; leveled; and ready for testing.
Required Inputs: Product specs; install kit; utilities condition; tools; code requirements.
Supporting Roles: Customer; dispatch; technical support; operations management
Systems / Tools: Product specs; install kit; tools; code guidance
Happy Path Summary: The crew places the unit; connects utilities; levels it; and reaches a test-ready state without code or safety issues.
Pain Point 1: Code or safety issue appears during hookup.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the issue is remediable within field authority.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Unsafe install; liability; callback; customer harm.
Pain Point 2: Product issue or missing install kit blocks connection.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether a field workaround is allowed or a revisit is required.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Incomplete job; repeat trip; customer frustration.
Pain Point 3: Utilities condition differs from what readiness review suggested.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether the variance is safe and within scope.
Common Exceptions: Missing kit/parts; outlet or hookup mismatch; placement constraint.
Rare Edge Cases: Code violation; safety hazard; defective product.
Escalation Trigger: Code; safety; or product issues appear.
Required Documentation: Install limitation; parts gap; code issue; reason codes; field install notes and stop disposition.
Proof Required: Photos; checklist; specific defect or code notes.

Objective: Verify the unit works and secure customer acceptance.
Completion Condition: Tests pass; customer is briefed; and sign-off is captured or an exception is documented.
Required Inputs: Completed install; test checklist; customer present; photos or notes.
Systems / Tools: Test checklist; photo capture; POD/signature tool
Happy Path Summary: The crew runs functional checks; explains the result to the customer; captures proof; and closes the stop cleanly.
Pain Point 1 Key Variable: Whether the failure is fixable immediately or requires handoff.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Repeat visit; complaint; low trust; unresolved safety or performance issue.
Pain Point 2: Customer disputes quality; damage; or completeness.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether evidence and scope are clear.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Escalation; claim; chargeback; poor recovery experience.
Pain Point 3: Sign-off or POD is not captured correctly.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether proof can still be captured before departure.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Billing/proof problems; dispute vulnerability; weak handoff to support.
Common Exceptions: Customer questions after test; incomplete documentation.
Rare Edge Cases: Hidden defect or damage discovered at sign-off.
Escalation Trigger: Dispute; damage; or failure cannot be resolved within the stop.
Required Documentation: Test result; sign-off status; photos; dispute notes; POD and completion status updates.
Proof Required: Photos; checklist result; signature or documented refusal.

Objective: Resolve defects; damage; incomplete work; or customer complaints quickly.
Start Trigger: An issue is reported or documented after the visit.
Completion Condition: Remedy is completed or ownership and timeline are handed off.
Required Inputs: Install notes; photos; reason codes; complaint details; service history.
Primary Decision Owner: Customer care / field service / operations management depending on severity and cost
Systems / Tools: Case notes; complaint details; service history; reason codes
Happy Path Summary: The issue is classified correctly; routed to the right owner; supported by good evidence; and either resolved or cleanly handed off with a timeline.
Pain Point 1 Cascade Risk: Delayed remedy; extra touches; angry customer; avoidable cost.
Pain Point 2: Evidence from the original stop is incomplete.
Pain Point 2 Key Variable: Whether the original crew notes and photos are sufficient to decide next action.
Pain Point 2 Cascade Risk: Rework; dispute; weak accountability; longer resolution time.
Pain Point 3: Ownership between customer care; field service; and ops is unclear.
Pain Point 3 Key Variable: Whether the issue is customer communication; field remedy; or management exception.
Pain Point 3 Cascade Risk: Dropped cases; duplicated work; poor recovery experience.
Common Exceptions: Minor complaints; incomplete work; evidence review.
Rare Edge Cases: Safety claim; major damage claim; severe product defect.
Escalation Trigger: Severity or cost exceeds front-line authority.
Required Documentation: Complaint details; service history; evidence; owner; promised timeline; case file and service record updates.
Proof Required: Install notes; photos; reason codes; service history.
KPI 2: Repeat visit rate / complaint recovery quality
